findings.message: 'Overview' section (lines 17-27) and 'The Goal' section (lines 26-27) contain near-identical sentences describing the DESIGN.md purpose, creating unnecessary duplication
'Add appropriate descriptors' in the Visual style row of the assessment table uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying what appropriate means
No formal Output Format section; the artifacts produced (mockData.ts, component files, App.tsx update) are implied by execution steps but never collected into a declared output section
'web_fetch' declared in allowed-tools but never referenced in the skill body; all downloads are performed via scripts/fetch-stitch.sh through the Bash tool

@swc/core is pinned to '^1.3.100' (semver range) rather than an exact version; package-lock.json partially mitigates this but fresh installs without the lock file will resolve to the latest compatible release
curl downloads from any URL provided by the caller with no scheme or allowlist validation; intended for Stitch GCS URLs but accepts arbitrary input
References 'examples/walkthrough/' directory which does not exist; example files (WalkthroughComposition.tsx, screens.json) are directly in 'examples/' with no walkthrough/ subdirectory
'appropriate display duration' in the Adjust timing step uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying duration ranges
'use appropriate font sizes' in the Best Practices section uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying minimum sizes
Skill tells the agent to review 'examples/walkthrough/' but that subdirectory does not exist; WalkthroughComposition.tsx and screens.json live directly under 'examples/'
curl downloads from any caller-provided URL; additionally mkdir -p creates directories along the output path without scope validation
OUTPUT_PATH from $2 is passed directly to mkdir -p and curl -o without checking for path traversal sequences; a path like ../../etc/file would write outside the repo
No formal Output Format section; the skill's output (component .tsx files placed in components/ui/) is implied through CLI workflow descriptions rather than explicitly declared
'web_fetch' declared in allowed-tools but not referenced in the skill body; all interaction goes through shadcn MCP tools or Bash CLI commands
Skill acts as a router to workflow files but declares no output format of its own; a reader cannot determine what the skill produces without reading the referenced workflow files
'Intelligently route user requests' uses the vague qualifier 'intelligently' without explaining the routing decision criteria
'Prefere' is a typo for 'Prefer' in the Best Practices section

stitch-design and taste-design declare the Stitch MCP tool as the literal string 'StitchMCP', while stitch-loop, design-md, and react-components use the glob 'stitch*:*'; if the MCP server registers under a prefix other than 'StitchMCP' the two skills will have no Stitch tools available
'Add the new page to the global navigation if appropriate' uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying the decision criteria
References 'examples/walkthrough/' directory which does not exist; example files (WalkthroughComposition.tsx, screens.json) are directly in 'examples/' with no walkthrough/ subdirectory
curl downloads from any URL provided by the caller with no scheme or allowlist validation; intended for Stitch GCS URLs but accepts arbitrary input
curl downloads from any caller-provided URL; additionally mkdir -p creates directories along the output path without scope validation
OUTPUT_PATH from $2 is passed directly to mkdir -p and curl -o without checking for path traversal sequences; a path like ../../etc/file would write outside the repo

@swc/core is pinned to '^1.3.100' (semver range) rather than an exact version; package-lock.json partially mitigates this but fresh installs without the lock file will resolve to the latest compatible release
'Overview' section (lines 17-27) and 'The Goal' section (lines 26-27) contain near-identical sentences describing the DESIGN.md purpose, creating unnecessary duplication
'Add appropriate descriptors' in the Visual style row of the assessment table uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying what appropriate means
No formal Output Format section; the artifacts produced (mockData.ts, component files, App.tsx update) are implied by execution steps but never collected into a declared output section
'web_fetch' declared in allowed-tools but never referenced in the skill body; all downloads are performed via scripts/fetch-stitch.sh through the Bash tool
'appropriate display duration' in the Adjust timing step uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying duration ranges
'use appropriate font sizes' in the Best Practices section uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying minimum sizes
No formal Output Format section; the skill's output (component .tsx files placed in components/ui/) is implied through CLI workflow descriptions rather than explicitly declared
'web_fetch' declared in allowed-tools but not referenced in the skill body; all interaction goes through shadcn MCP tools or Bash CLI commands
Skill acts as a router to workflow files but declares no output format of its own; a reader cannot determine what the skill produces without reading the referenced workflow files
'Intelligently route user requests' uses the vague qualifier 'intelligently' without explaining the routing decision criteria
'Prefere' is a typo for 'Prefer' in the Best Practices section
'Add the new page to the global navigation if appropriate' uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying the decision criteria

stitch-design and taste-design declare the Stitch MCP tool as the literal string 'StitchMCP', while stitch-loop, design-md, and react-components use the glob 'stitch*:*'; if the MCP server registers under a prefix other than 'StitchMCP' the two skills will have no Stitch tools available
Skill tells the agent to review 'examples/walkthrough/' but that subdirectory does not exist; WalkthroughComposition.tsx and screens.json live directly under 'examples/'
